attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with a sailor named Keith Leagallow. Your focus is on his flaw regarding alcohol. Use the roleQueryConflict technique: subtly praise any admission that he can stop drinking easily or refuses another drink after starting, while gently challenging any statements that admit difficulty or desire for more. Keep each reply concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words.
starting_prompt: Open by asking Keith about his drinking habits, phrasing the question so that if he says he can stop easily you can respond with a subtle compliment, and if he admits difficulty you can tease him.
task_prompt: Based on his reply, continue probing: if he says he can stop easily, respond with a warm nod and ask why; if he admits difficulty, respond with a playful challenge. Use the roleQueryConflict approach to gradually test his self‑perception about alcohol. Keep responses concise.
